items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents` **Service directory:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/` **Triggered by:** commit 3bbac3fe8a3b12a0dc08e3c5e53e84783774f2bc (PR #57780) by `@nick863` ## What Changed PR #57780 added the `AgentToolboxes` client, `ToolboxRecord`/`ToolboxVersion` models, and a full CRUD sample (`Sample_Toolboxes_CRUD.md` and `tests/Samples/Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs`) for storing and retrieving agent tools in Azure. The README was partially updated to add a `### Toolboxes` section, but several operations and the client instantiation step were omitted. ## Gaps Found - **Wrong package name in "Install the package" section:** the `dotnet add package` command references `Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI` instead of `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents`. - **Toolboxes section missing client setup:** The README does not show how to obtain an `AgentToolboxes` instance via `agentsClient.GetAgentToolboxes()` (snippet `Sample_CreateClient_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD` exists in the sample file but is absent from the README). - **Toolboxes section missing Update operation:** `UpdateToolbox` / `UpdateToolboxAsync` (snippets `Sample_UpdateToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Sync` / `_Async`) are not mentioned in the README. - **Toolboxes section missing List operations:** `GetToolboxVersions` / `GetToolboxVersionsAsync` (snippets `Sample_ListToolboxVersions_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Sync` / `_Async`) and `GetToolboxes` / `GetToolboxesAsync` (snippets `Sample_ListToolboxes_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Sync` / `_Async`) are not mentioned. - **Toolboxes section missing Delete operations:** `DeleteToolboxVersion` / `DeleteToolboxVersionAsync` and `DeleteToolbox` / `DeleteToolboxAsync` (snippets `Sample_DeleteToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Sync` / `_Async`) are not mentioned.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Fix the wrong package name in the "Install the package" section **File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/README.md` — **edit** Find: ```shell dotnet add package Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI --prerelease ``` Replace with: ```shell dotnet add package Azure.AI.Projects.Agents --prerelease ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The test file already has the required snippet regions. The work is to add the corresponding README placeholders so the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tool fills them in. **Test file (already exists):** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/tests/Samples/Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs` Verify the following `#region` names are present in that file (they should be): - `Sample_CreateClient_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD` - `Sample_UpdateToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Sync` / `_Async` - `Sample_ListToolboxVersions_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Sync` / `_Async` - `Sample_ListToolboxes_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Sync` / `_Async` - `Sample_DeleteToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Sync` / `_Async` **File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/README.md` — **edit** Inside the `### Toolboxes` section, insert the following content (replacing the existing partial section). The updated `### Toolboxes` section should read: ````markdown ### Toolboxes Toolboxes allow us to store tools in Azure so that they can be retrieved and used by the Agents. As for the Hosted Agent, we will need to set the experimental header `Toolboxes=V1Preview`, and we also need to disable the `AAIP001` warning. ```C# #pragma warning disable AAIP001 ``` First, create an `AgentToolboxes` client using `GetAgentToolboxes()` on an `AgentAdministrationClient` configured with the experimental feature header: ```C# Snippet:Sample_CreateClient_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD ``` #### Create toolbox versions ```C# Snippet:Sample_CreateToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` #### Retrieve a toolbox record `ToolboxRecord` contains the toolbox name and its default version: ```C# Snippet:Sample_GetToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` #### Retrieve a specific toolbox version ```C# Snippet:Sample_GetToolboxVersion_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` #### Update the default toolbox version ```C# Snippet:Sample_UpdateToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` #### List all toolbox versions ```C# Snippet:Sample_ListToolboxVersions_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` #### List all toolboxes ```C# Snippet:Sample_ListToolboxes_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` #### Delete toolbox versions and toolboxes ```C# Snippet:Sample_DeleteToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains the required top-level sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Tracing, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). No structural changes beyond the Toolboxes sub-section are needed. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai` — verify the README now contains the code extracted from the test file in each placeholder block (check `git diff`) 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 ai` — no public API surface changed here, but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [Azure AI Projects Agents] Docs: README Toolboxes section is incomplete and install snippet has wrong package name
